D10 Imaging L5 Evaluate Reference

3D LUT — Cube Sampling & Trilinear Interpolation

a 3D LUT is a tiny cube of pre-computed colours; trilinear interpolation between the 8 corner cells maps every input RGB to its output

A 3D LUT samples an N×N×N grid in RGB space. To map any input RGB the engine locates the input inside its enclosing voxel and trilinearly interpolates the 8 corner values. Resolution (33³ typical) sets the precision; coarser grids show banding on gradients.

A simple "warm look" LUT is applied. Drag the resolution slider — at 4³ you see obvious banding in the gradient; at 33³ the LUT is smooth. Cube visualisation shows the 8 corner colours surrounding the slider point.
